paper_title: Longevity and Temperature Effects on Chironomids in Geothermal Streams

methods.method_name: Field Collection and Lab Study of Chironomids
methods.description: This method describes the collection of adult chironomids from geothermal streams and their subsequent laboratory incubation to study the effects of temperature on their longevity.

steps.description: Uninjured and recently emerged adult chironomids were collected using 8 close-weave muslin emergence traps, 3 on IS7 and 5 on IS8.
steps.searchable_summary: collect adult chironomids using emergence traps

steps.description: Adult chironomids were collected individually into one-dram snap top vials using an aspirator and transported to the laboratory in a cooler.
steps.searchable_summary: transport chironomids to lab in vials

steps.description: A small amount of stream water was added to each vial during collection to prevent desiccation.

Adults were sorted by sex to have equal numbers of each sex within each treatment, and then were randomly assigned to one of two treatment groups for incubation at either constant 6°C or constant 20°C.
steps.searchable_summary: sort chironomids by sex and assign to temperature treatments

steps.description: Adults were assessed daily for survival using the protocol outlined in Ferrington (2019).
Individuals not exhibiting physical movement when the vials were tapped were judged as dead and preserved in 90% EtOH.
The experiment was terminated at 32 days. All individuals still alive on day 32 were sacrificed by preserving them in 90% ETOH.
steps.searchable_summary: terminate experiment and preserve remaining chironomids

methods.description: This method involves the identification of chironomid specimens to genus level and the analysis of their longevity data using statistical models.

steps.description: Adults were identified to genus following preservation using Weiderholm (1989).
Longevities of Diamesa were analyzed at the genus-level because females could not be unambiguously differentiated to species.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze Diamesa longevity at genus level

steps.description: Temperature effects on longevity of Diamesa adults were analyzed using a generalized linear mixed-effects model (GLME) with a Poisson distribution from the lme4 package in RStudio.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GLME analysis on Diamesa longevity

steps.description: Residuals for the GLME were more evenly distributed after removing outliers.
Visualization of treatment effects was performed using ggplot2.
Mean longevity was calculated based on time from retrieval in the emergence traps to death in the laboratory for all individuals that died during incubation.
Survival estimates for Diamesa were calculated using the Kaplan-Meier nonparametric estimation approach.
